text:
What possessed the British Olympic Association to think it could “rebrand” the British national flag with some multicoloured nonsense that consists of squiggles, dots and vivid shades of pink and purple...?

Why mess with a national flag that is instantly recognisable and respected around the world?

Or does the British Olympic Association think the British flag isn’t good enough for the British Olympic Association? Is it ashamed of our national flag and what it signifies? Because if it is then it shouldn’t be called the British Olympic Association.

I’m guessing they were trying to be “inclusive” with this pretend flag when what they were actually doing was insulting an emblem and a flag that past generations have fought and died for.

Anyway, what kind of re-branded emblem could be better than the ACTUAL Union Jack – a flag that people in the remotest corners of the globe recognise.

It’s starting to feel like certain people in certain institutions are determined to erase Britishness and stamp it out - to make it like it never existed because those people are ashamed of this country and everything it stands for. These are the same people for whom patriotism equals racism. To them being a patriot is being a racist. Which is ludicrous considering we live in one of the most diverse, inclusive, and tolerant countries on God’s green earth.

Remember when a string of Union Jacks were unfurled across Regent Street on Jubilee weekend in 2022 Remainiacs went into meltdown? The hashtag #Nazi Germany was trending and India Willoughby, Britain’s first trans journalist and TV newsreader, declared: “When the Queen dies I wouldn’t be surprised if Boris appoints himself Fuhrer and assumes total control. That’s how close I think Britain is to Nazi Germany.”

Eh?????

And close behind her was Professor Paul Bernal at UEA who suggested ‘the only things that are missing are the tanks.’

What anti-British, abject nonsense. What kind of mind has THAT reaction to our national flag – especially when those particular flags were to celebrate the Queen’s 70 years on the throne?

Don't miss:Vote on if UK should introduce a minister to protect flag after Team GB outrage

Don't miss:Nigel Farage erupts at rebrand to Team GB's Union Jack

Don't miss:Team GB slammed for 'insulting' decision to change Union flag to pink and purple

Don't miss:Labour fury against Keir Starmer over Union Jacks on election leaflets

And yes, some of those people were young. And while this is not a blanket condemnation of them they do seem to be the people who see no good in our country, only fault. Who outrageously see the British flag as being representative of fascism.

And it was to these people that the British Olympic Association was pandering to when it decided to rebrand the British flag because it thought “young people” would find it boring.

Well tough if they do. Should we change everything the young find boring or don’t relate to? Because if we did there would be nothing left.

Or should we maybe expect - hope - they might try to understand what the British flag signifies to many?

Could they perhaps try to understand those brave people who fought for this flag and died while protecting it? Could they try to understand what the flag means to the families of our fallen?

And could they try to understand that patriotism isn’t about zealotry, fascism or racism - it’s about love and affection for the country you live in?

Shame on The British Olympic Association for trying to jazz up the Union Jack to get the attention of the young. And the backlash that has resulted from its stupidity and its naivety is well deserved.

EvenRishi Sunaksays national flags are a source of pride that really shouldn’t be messed with.

So now, after the backlash, the BOA is - quite rightly – back peddling. It says the Union Jack WILL be emblazoned across the arms of Team GB athletes at the Paris Olympics this summer.

And it promised the controversial new colour scheme would just appear on a selection of the team merchandise.

We use your sign-up to provide content in ways you've consented to and to improve our understanding of you. This may include adverts from us and 3rd parties based on our understanding. You can unsubscribe at any time. Read ourPrivacy Policy

But Fatima Whitbread - the former javelin world champ- is furious: “I’m absolutely disgusted to think they’ve done it. Let’s face it, it represents our late Queen and it represents everything that embraces what’s good about this country”

And she’s right.

The only people who think this re-brand is a good idea are people who don’t love or care about Britain. People who have no affection for it, who can only look to the wrongs of the past and condemn the Britain of today for them.

Malcolm Farrow the president of the Flag Institute says: "People have every right to be upset with Team GB. Changing the flag is bordering on an insult to all those who died protecting it!"

And he’s right too.

The idea of changing our national flag to placate bored young people IS an insult. But then these young people have never had to fight in a war. They’ve never had to fight and die for their freedoms. And they appear to have little or no empathy, gratitude or respect for the people who did.

As for all the rest who think the Union Jack is about fascism and racism, I’d say it’s their twisted minds that are the problem here.

The people who care about their flag and their country are the people who actually like Britain and are the most likely to want to help improve it, contribute to it, and make it better.

title: Changing the Union flag is a gift to those who hate our country | Express Comment | Comment | Express.co.uk
author: Carole Malone
description: It's starting to feel like certain people are doing everything they can to erase our Britishness, says Carole Malone.
